title: Bring up a peripheral from the datasheet
description: Initialize a memory-mapped peripheral by following the reference manual's exact register sequence, verifying each step on hardware.
body: Steps: 1. Read the reference manual section for the peripheral and note the required init order (clock enable, reset, config, enable). 2. Enable the peripheral clock and release reset first — many writes are ignored until clocked. 3. Configure registers in the documented order, respecting read-modify-write and write-once fields. 4. Verify each step by reading back status/ready bits, not by assuming the write took. 5. Exercise the peripheral and confirm with a logic analyzer/scope. Rollback point: before enabling interrupts from the peripheral. Validation: status bits confirm configuration and the bus signals match expectation on a scope; a write that 'looks right' but was issued before the clock was enabled silently does nothing.
